changes_made: Changed k8s_audit_logging_enabled to k8s_apiserver_audit_logging_enabled for resource specificity
review_notes: The functions generally map well to PCI DSS 8.2.1, focusing on encryption and audit logging. However, the naming of 'k8s_audit_logging_enabled' needed refinement to specify the API server context, aligning with existing naming conventions. The other functions are well-named and technically accurate.
naming_improvements: Use 'apiserver' instead of 'audit' for resource name in audit logging function
title: Requirement 8.2.1
step1_reasoning: The PCI DSS 8.2.1 control focuses on encryption and secure handling of sensitive data. In Kubernetes, this maps to ensuring that secrets are encrypted at rest, etcd (which stores cluster data) is encrypted, and audit logs are enabled and retained to track access and changes. These controls ensure that sensitive information is protected and access is monitored.
